topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the lecture hall seemed to intensify, casting a sickly yellow glow on the sheaf of papers clutched in my sweaty hands. When Professor Albright read the accusation—a carefully worded indictment of intellectual theft—the world tilted. My vision narrowed to the texture of the cheap laminate desk in front of me. A strange, hollow sensation bloomed in my chest, a sudden inability to process the sheer absurdity of the situation. I could feel the blood draining from my face, leaving a cold, clammy residue. My mouth went dry, and I could only manage a faint, reedy sound when the professor asked for my response. Every word I tried to form felt like sand slipping through my fingers.

[story 2]

"You're claiming you copied directly from the source material, Mark?" The accusation hung in the air, sharp and undeniable. I stared at the accusation written on the screen, the words swimming before my eyes, refusing to coalesce into meaning. My hands started to tremble uncontrollably, the tremor radiating up my arms and making it hard to hold my pen. I blinked rapidly, trying to force my brain to catch up to the sudden tidal wave of disbelief. A strange silence descended around me, thick and suffocating, as if the air itself had stopped moving. I just sat there, my jaw slack, unable to formulate a defense, every nerve ending screaming in protest.

[story 3]

The accusation landed with a sickening thud in the quiet corner of the student union. I looked up at Sarah, expecting anger, but what I saw was a vacant stare. My mind felt utterly blank, a vast, echoing emptiness where logic should have been. I fiddled with the frayed edge of my notebook, the motion jerky and uncoordinated. When Sarah finally spoke, her voice was level, almost clinical, which only amplified my internal confusion. I could hear nothing but a high-pitched ringing, and my feet felt rooted to the floor. I just blinked slowly, processing the reality of the situation as if watching a poorly dubbed film.
